events.title: US Blockade of Strait of Hormuz Begins
events.desc: The United States has initiated a naval blockade around Iran's ports, a move Iran has condemned as a 'grave violation' of its sovereignty. This action escalates tensions amidst a fragile truce and follows protests in Tehran against the blockade.

events.title: Israel Intensifies Strikes on Lebanon Amidst Looming Talks
events.desc: Israel has significantly increased its strikes on Lebanon, resulting in over 160 fatalities since Friday. This escalation continues despite a US-Iran ceasefire and ahead of scheduled peace talks between Lebanon and Israel in Washington.

events.title: Hezbollah Leader Urges Lebanon to Cancel Talks with Israel
events.desc: Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem has called on the Lebanese government to withdraw from planned peace talks with Israel in Washington, labeling them as pointless. He asserted that Hezbollah would continue to confront Israeli actions against Lebanon.

events.title: US-Iran Talks Conclude Without Breakthrough, Dialogue Continues
events.desc: High-level talks between the US and Iran in Islamabad have concluded without a definitive breakthrough to end the prolonged conflict. Despite the lack of immediate agreement, sources indicate that channels for dialogue remain open.

events.title: UN Urges Respect for Navigation in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United Nations Secretary-General has issued a call for all parties involved to uphold the freedom of navigation within the Strait of Hormuz, emphasizing the importance of maintaining open maritime routes amidst escalating tensions.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of Piracy Amidst Hormuz Blockade
events.desc: Iran has formally accused the United States of engaging in piracy due to the imposed blockade around its ports. This accusation comes as thousands gathered in Tehran to protest the US actions, further inflaming the already tense situation.

events.title: Israel Strikes Gaza; UN Experts Condemn Attacks
events.desc: Israeli forces conducted strikes in Gaza, resulting in the deaths of three Palestinians. Concurrently, UN experts have strongly criticized attacks on shelters and instances of forced displacement in the West Bank, highlighting ongoing violence against Palestinian civilians.

events.title: Iran's Asymmetric Counterair Campaign Targets US Air Bases
events.desc: Recent Iranian drone and missile attacks on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia destroyed an E-3 Sentry and damaged KC-135 tankers. This is part of a broader Iranian counterair strategy aimed at disrupting US air operations in the region.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: Iran Imposes Internet Blackout Amidst Hostilities
events.desc: During recent Israeli and American strikes, the Iranian government implemented a near-total internet blackout, reducing traffic by 98 percent. This action was reportedly aimed at controlling information flow to the Iranian populace rather than as a defensive measure against external attacks.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their corresponding passwords have been leaked online. The breach affects 12 out of 13 ministries, potentially exposing sensitive information due to weak security protocols.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel Officials Set for US-Mediated Talks
events.desc: Ambassadors from Lebanon and Israel are scheduled to meet in Washington D.C. for State Department-led talks. This diplomatic engagement aims to address the ongoing conflict, although uncertainty surrounds the discussions due to continued Israeli bombardment.

events.title: US Vice President Vance Claims Progress in Talks with Iran
events.desc: U.S. Vice President JD Vance stated that significant progress was made during recent talks with Iran, asserting that the next steps are now contingent on Tehran's actions, particularly regarding the opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Summon Iraqi Diplomats Over Militia Attacks
events.desc: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia have summoned Iraqi diplomats in response to attacks linked to Iran-backed militias operating within Iraq. The United States and Kuwait have also voiced criticism regarding Iraq's handling of these groups.
events.location: Manama, Bahrain / Riyadh, Saudi Arabia / Baghdad, Iraq

events.title: UN Warns of Agrifood Catastrophe from Hormuz Crisis
events.desc: The United Nations' Food and Agriculture Organization has cautioned that a prolonged crisis in the Strait of Hormuz could lead to a global agrifood catastrophe. Disruptions to fertilizer and energy exports are predicted to drive up food prices and reduce crop yields, disproportionately affecting poorer nations.

events.title: US Considers Blocking Weapons Sales to Israel
events.desc: Senator Bernie Sanders has vowed to push a resolution aimed at blocking US weapons sales to Israel. While passage in the Republican-controlled Senate is unlikely, the move signifies a potential shift in support for Israel among some Democrats.
